reasoning: The Chicago Fed ANFCI index has exhibited a strong, accelerating upward trend (signaling tightening financial conditions) over the past six weeks, increasing from -0.588 on Jan 16 to -0.515 on Feb 27. The weekly increases have steadily accelerated (+0.006, +0.012, +0.015, +0.019, +0.020). This trend coincides with early February news of a US bank collapse and general market volatility (tech ETFs down 10% in Feb). Given the strong short-term momentum, there is a high probability (~77%) that this tightening trend will persist into the first resolution date (Mar 8). By the second resolution date (Mar 31), the probability decreases (~65%) as mean reversion could occur or the Federal Reserve may intervene if tightening progresses too rapidly, although reaching below the early March baseline within just a month still seems somewhat less likely given the trajectory.
